title: Pushing to TLS registry with non-standard port fails
body:
/kind bug
**Description**
I would like to run a registry on a high port (e.g. localhost:5443) during testing.

But pushing to this fails, as part of Podman's communication drops the port and just uses the domain name.

FWIW this works using non-TLS registry running on e.g. localhost:5000.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run a SSL-enabled registry on a non-standard port.
    Use this script to do it (rename to .sh and run it).
    [podman-registry-ssl.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/5297321/podman-registry-ssl.txt)

2. podman pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest

3. podman tag docker.io/library/alpine:latest localhost:5443/library/alpine:latest

4. podman --log-level=debug push --tls-verify=false localhost:5443/library/alpine:latest

**Describe the results you received:**

```console
$ podman --log-level=debug push --tls-verify=false localhost:5443/library/alpine:latest
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would expect the PATCH line to use the same port as the POST line:

```
DEBU[0000] POST https://localhost:5443/v2/library/alpine/blobs/uploads/ 
DEBU[0000] PATCH https://localhost/v2/library/alpine/blobs/uploads/...
```

comments.body:
AFAIK, everything else than 5000 requires TLS.

It's at least not a Podman issue. Running the registry with Docker behaves exactly the same. I am a horrible sys admin, so others may know why 5000 is special. @edsantiago @mtrmac ?
As it's not a Podman issue, I am going to close but we can continue the conversation.

The registry does run with TLS.

The --tls-verify=false option is solely there, because of the comment in the troubleshooting guide about authentication.
The PATCH URL comes directly from a `Location` header sent by the server, so the server is misconfigured and does not know its own port. The `-p 5443:443` might have something to do with it.
@mtrmac Ah! I am indeed running it without configuration. I will investigate, cheers!

> The registry does run with TLS.
> 
> The --tls-verify=false option is solely there, because of the comment in the troubleshooting guide about authentication.

Thanks! I'll grab a new coffee.

It looks like something's odd in the setup. We test registries+TLS in every run of our CI (see the script https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/hack/podman-registry).

From a Podman / c/image point of view, the thing to check is just running the upstream registry server on 5443 . If that works, and the `nginx` proxy doesn’t, my starting hypothesis would be that the `nginx` proxy is not implementing the protocol as expected.

@mtrmac I can indeed push to docker://localhost:5444 (just to pick a non-standard port) when there is no TLS.

I must need some magic in the nginx configuration.

Thanks for time+input (all of you).

(When I find the cause, I will update here, so it is not an empty trail, should anyone go the same way)

The debug output now contains parts like (PATH fixed):

```
DEBU[0000] Uploading /v2/library/alpine/blobs/uploads/  
DEBU[0000] POST https://localhost:5443/v2/library/alpine/blobs/uploads/ 
DEBU[0000] PATCH https://localhost:5443/v2/library/alpine/blobs/uploads/17e40225-58e9-42b9-a019-db811ec38492?_state=YTJ8djV9Iqt3OFFISuxLo5yUglANjH2jas5dQScEyip7Ik5hbWUiOiJsaWJyYXJ5L2FscGluZSIsIlVVSUQiOiIxN2U0MDIyNS01OGU5LTQyYjktYTAxOS1kYjgxMWVjMzg0OTIiLCJPZmZzZXQiOjAsIlN0YXJ0ZWRBdCI6IjIwMjAtMDktMzBUMTI6NDI6NDguNTE1NTAzMDE2WiJ9 
Copying blob 50644c29ef5a done  
```
